input_prompt: A freelance invoicing tool with time tracking and automated payment reminders via email and SMS

contract.tagline: Invoice instantly. Get paid automatically. Never chase again.
contract.problem_statement: Freelancers spend an average of 8 hours per month chasing overdue invoices, and 29% of invoices are paid late, costing U.S. freelancers $50B in delayed cash flow annually. Tools like QuickBooks are too complex and priced for businesses, while Wave and PayPal invoices lack automated follow-up sequences and integrated time tracking.
target_users: Independent freelancers billing 3-15 clients monthly earning $5K-$20K/month
Boutique agencies with 2-8 staff managing 20-50 active projects
Fractional executives and consultants billing hourly or on retainer
Creative professionals — designers, writers, videographers — with irregular billing

core_features.name: One-Click Invoice Generation
core_features.description: Generate professional branded invoices from tracked time in under 10 seconds. Auto-populate client data, rate, and hours.
core_features.user_story: As a freelancer, I want to convert my time log to an invoice in one click so that billing takes seconds, not an hour.

core_features.name: Automated Payment Chase Sequences
core_features.description: Smart reminder sequences: email at due date, SMS at +3 days, final notice at +7 days. Stops automatically on payment.
core_features.user_story: As a freelancer, I want reminders sent automatically so that I never have to awkwardly chase a client again.

core_features.name: Integrated Time Tracker
core_features.description: Browser-based and mobile timer with project tagging, billable/non-billable toggle, and detailed time reports.
core_features.user_story: As a consultant, I want to track time by project so that I can bill accurately and justify my hours to clients.

core_features.name: Stripe & ACH Payments
core_features.description: Clients pay directly from the invoice link via credit card or ACH bank transfer. Funds deposited in 1-2 days.
core_features.user_story: As a freelancer, I want clients to pay right from the invoice so that collection requires zero friction.

core_features.name: Client Portal
core_features.description: Password-free client portal where clients view all invoices, download PDFs, and manage payment methods.
core_features.user_story: As a client, I want a self-service portal so that I can find invoices without emailing my freelancer.

core_features.name: Revenue Analytics Dashboard
core_features.description: Monthly revenue charts, average payment time per client, overdue invoice heatmap, and projected monthly income.
core_features.user_story: As a freelancer, I want income visibility so that I can plan my finances month to month.

steps: Signs up with email in 30 seconds, no credit card required
Starts time tracker on first client project
Stops timer after 4.5 hours and clicks 'Create Invoice' — auto-populated
Sends invoice; client pays via card link in 24 hours
user_journeys.success_condition: First invoice paid within 48 hours of signup with zero manual follow-up

api_endpoints.description: Create new invoice, optionally from time entries
List all invoices with status and aging
Log or stop a time entry
Monthly revenue summary and client payment timing stats

contract.revenue_model: Freemium with 1.5% transaction fee on free tier + Pro ($19/mo) and Agency ($79/mo) subscriptions. At 10,000 Pro users: $2.28M ARR. Transaction fee from free tier adds ~$200K ARR additional.
contract.go_to_market: SEO-led content marketing targeting 'how to invoice freelance', 'chase invoice email template' (50K+ monthly searches). Partner with Toptal, Fiverr Pro, and Upwork Pro for co-marketing. Chrome extension for time tracking drives daily active usage and virality.
known_risks: Stripe charges 2.9% + 30¢ per transaction — after our 1.5% fee, margin on free tier payments is negative at low invoice amounts
Wave (free) and PayPal are entrenched free alternatives that are good enough for many freelancers
SMS delivery requires Twilio trust score — new accounts face high delivery failure rates during warmup
Targeting freelancers means high churn during economic downturns when freelance income drops

generated_files.backend/main.py: # Billdr FastAPI backend — generated by PROMETHEUS
generated_files.README.md:
# Billdr

Invoice instantly. Get paid automatically.

kill_reports.title: Negative Unit Economics on Free Tier
kill_reports.description: Stripe charges 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction. Billing a 1.5% platform fee means at a $100 invoice, Stripe costs $3.20 and you collect $1.50 — a $1.70 loss per transaction. You are literally paying for free users to get paid. Wave, the main competitor, survives this only because their 2.9% processing fee is the revenue, not a cost. At scale, 10,000 free tier invoices at $500 average = $85,000 in Stripe fees vs $75,000 in platform fees collected. Net burn: -$10,000/month.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Raise free tier platform fee to 3.5% (above Stripe cost) or eliminate it and use the free tier purely as a conversion funnel with a hard 5 invoice/month cap to force Pro upgrade. Model after invoice.ninja.
kill_reports.patch_description: Restructured free tier to a hard 5 invoice/month cap with no platform fee, eliminating the negative margin transaction model and converting free tier into a pure upgrade funnel targeting Pro at $19/mo.

kill_reports.title: SMS Reminder TCPA Violation — No Opt-In Collected
kill_reports.description: The Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA) requires explicit written consent before sending automated SMS messages. The current design sends SMS reminders to clients without collecting TCPA-compliant opt-in consent on the invoice. Class action TCPA lawsuits average $500-$1,500 per violation per plaintiff. 100 clients receiving one unsolicited SMS = $150,000 potential exposure.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Add 'Allow SMS reminders' checkbox to invoice creation flow. Store opt-in timestamp, IP, and user agent. Add SMS opt-out via 'Reply STOP' handling. Default all new clients to email-only until explicit SMS consent is given.
kill_reports.patch_description: Added TCPA-compliant SMS consent checkbox to invoice creation, stored consent audit trail with timestamp and IP in a dedicated consent_records table, implemented STOP reply handling via Twilio webhook, and defaulted all clients to email-only notifications.

kill_reports.title: No Mobile App — Core Use Case is On-the-Go
kill_reports.description: Freelancers start and stop timers between client calls, at coffee shops, and on job sites. A browser-only time tracker means they must remember to log hours retroactively — defeating the entire product promise. Toggl Track, Harvest, and Clockify all have iOS/Android apps as their primary interface. Launching without mobile puts Billdr at a structural disadvantage for the exact use case that creates daily habit.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Ship a Progressive Web App (PWA) with offline time tracking on day one. Add a one-tap floating timer widget via the Web Share Target API. Queue time entries locally and sync when back online. Full native apps in v2.
kill_reports.patch_description: Implemented PWA with service worker offline caching for time tracking, one-tap floating timer accessible from the home screen shortcut, and background sync for time entry uploads when connectivity is restored.

kill_reports.title: GDPR: No Data Residency for EU Freelancer Client Data
kill_reports.description: Freelancers storing EU client invoices on US-based infrastructure (Railway, Supabase US region) without a Data Processing Agreement violates GDPR Article 44's restrictions on cross-border data transfers. A single EU client complaint to a DPA can trigger a GDPR investigation. Penalties up to 4% global annual revenue or €20M.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Add EU data residency option on signup (Supabase EU region). Generate standard contractual clauses (SCCs) automatically for EU users. Add privacy policy disclosing data processors and transfer mechanisms.
kill_reports.patch_description: Added EU data residency toggle routing EU users to Supabase Frankfurt region, auto-generated Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) for EU user onboarding, and updated privacy policy to disclose all subprocessors and transfer mechanisms.

kill_reports.title: Celery Reminder Jobs Have No Idempotency — Duplicate SMS Risk
kill_reports.description: If the Celery worker crashes mid-execution and retries, the reminder task will resend the same SMS/email. Clients receiving 3 identical 'Your invoice is overdue' emails in 10 minutes will mark the domain as spam, destroying email deliverability and triggering client complaints. Twilio has no built-in deduplication for SMS sends.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Add idempotency keys to every reminder task stored in Redis with 24-hour TTL. Check key existence before sending. Add Celery task deduplication middleware. Store reminder_sent_at timestamp on invoice and check before every dispatch.
kill_reports.patch_description: Implemented idempotency keys for all reminder Celery tasks stored in Redis with 24-hour TTL, added pre-send duplicate check querying the reminder_log table, and configured Celery max_retries=1 with exponential backoff on reminder tasks.

kill_reports.title: Low Switching Cost Means Constant Threat from Wave/QuickBooks
kill_reports.description: Freelancer financial tools have near-zero switching costs. A freelancer can export invoices from Billdr, import to Wave (free), and cancel in under 30 minutes. Without a data moat or deep integrations, the product is only as sticky as the UX — and free competitors exist.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Build switching costs through: (1) client relationship data that clients come to rely on their portal, (2) historical time tracking data and reports that are valuable and non-exportable in Wave, (3) deep calendar and Notion integrations that make Billdr the central work hub.
kill_reports.challenge_argument: Wave has been 'free forever' since 2010 and has 4 million users — yet Harvest ($12/mo), FreshBooks ($19/mo), and invoice.ninja Pro all have thriving paid userbases of 200K+ each. Freelancers demonstrably pay for superior UX and time savings over free alternatives. Our PWA time tracker, one-click invoicing, and automated chase sequences create daily habit loops that Wave's clunky web-only UI cannot match. The real moat is earned through product quality, not feature gating.
